creator: bf2006a@yahoo.com
text:
Thanks for looking into it.  I'm not running a
tinderbox, so I didn't check it for all
platforms/versions.  If necessary, while you are
investigating, you could always make it available but
mark it broken for 5.x, as there are other lisps
available in FreeBSD ports for 5.x (which I think is
scheduled to reach security support end-of-life on May
31, anyway).

bf.
--- Felippe de Meirelles Motta <lippemail@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Em Fri, 21 Mar 2008 17:16:54 -0700 (PDT)
> bf <bf2006a@yahoo.com> escreveu:
> 
> > Please consider the attached patch in lieu of
> those
> > that have been submitted. It updates sbcl to
> 1.0.15,
> > and enables the futex-emulation code if
> > WITH_THREADS=yes, since we don't have native
> futexes
> > in FreeBSD (outside of the Linuxulator, anyway). 
> I
> > didn't rework the PORTDOCS like the previous
> committer
> > did, because I didn't see a point in installing
> html
> > documentation that is just a copy of the info
> files
> > that were already being installed: if someone
> wants
> > it, they can always do the conversion themselves. 
> And
> > building and installing the pdf or postscript
> docs,
> > also redundant, would require a dependency on
> teTeX. 
> > The only useful thing I can see in this regard
> would
> > be to implement the NOPORTDOCS wrapper, probably
> by
> > adding a (disable :sb-doc) line to
> > files/customize-target-features.lisp, 
> > or by commenting out the appropriate line in
> > base-target-features.lisp-expr with sed, and then
> > fixing the pkg-plist.  I didn't do it because it
> > seemed more trouble than it was worth.
> > 
> > bf.
> > 
> > 
> > 
> >      
>
____________________________________________________________________________________

> > Find them fast with Yahoo! Search.
> >
>
http://tools.search.yahoo.com/newsearch/category.php?category=shopping
> 
> Hi bf,
> 
> For some reason, this port doesn't extract and build
> on 5-FreeBSD, I'm
> investigating him.
> 
> -- 
> lippe@FreeBSD.org
> Felippe de Meirelles Motta
>
